consumer_electronics.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
consumer_electronics.summary: OpenAI is launching Daybreak, an AI initiative focused on detecting and patching vulnerabilities before attackers find them. Daybreak uses the Codex Security AI agent that launched in March to create a threat model based on an organization's code and focus on possible attack paths, validate likely vulnerabilities, and

consumer_electronics.title: Yarbo says it will remove the intentional backdoor from its robot lawn mower
consumer_electronics.summary: The company behind the robot lawn mower that ran me over has changed its tune. Yarbo now plans to completely remove the remote backdoor access that could have let bad actors reprogram the robot over the internet. Yarbo customers will be able to decide whether that feature even gets installed in the first place, co-foun

consumer_electronics.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
consumer_electronics.summary: Thinking Machines, the AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, announced Monday that it's working on something called "interaction models." The idea behind interaction models, according to Thinking Machines, is that they will let people "collaborate with AI the way we naturally collaborate with each other

consumer_electronics.title: Govee’s new portable smart lamp is on sale for the first time
consumer_electronics.summary: If you’ve always liked the idea of the Philips Hue Go portable lamp but couldn’t justify the $100+ price tag, Govee recently released a much cheaper alternative, the Govee Table Lamp Classic, and it’s already receiving its first discount. Right now, you can pick the rechargeable smart lamp up at Amazon for $63.99 ($16

consumer_electronics.title: Palantir’s true believers are wearing this jacket
consumer_electronics.summary: In late April, Palantir - the software company that, in recent years, has perhaps become best known for its defense industry contracts and work with Immigration and Customs Enforcement - announced that it would be adding new products to its merch store. The latest offering was a cotton chore coat. At $239 and in bright

consumer_electronics.title: Apple brings encrypted RCS chats to iPhone
consumer_electronics.summary: Apple now lets you have encrypted RCS conversations with Android users through the Messages app on iOS. As part of iOS 26.5, which was released on Monday, Apple added support for end-to-end encrypted RCS messaging in beta, meaning that Apple and Google can't see your messages while they're sent. With iOS 26.5, you'll s

consumer_electronics.title: Texas sues Netflix for advertising ‘bait and switch’ and spying
consumer_electronics.summary: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit against Netflix, accusing the company of turning its back on its promise to remain ad-free and safe for kids. The lawsuit, filed on Monday, claims Netflix has "opened Texans' data for inspection by the same Big Ad Tech community it once criticized for exploiting use

consumer_electronics.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
consumer_electronics.summary: For the first time, Google says it has spotted and stopped a zero-day exploit developed with AI. consumer_electronics.title: A million baby monitors and security cameras were easily viewable by hackers
consumer_electronics.summary: A baby's eyes peer directly into the camera lens. A kid with a striped shirt looks up, then away. A boy in a policeman's costume, a gold star on his chest. A messy bedroom that reminds me of my own daughters, with an unmade bunk bed, a little girl's hat and headband, and Hello Kitty […]

consumer_electronics.title: 7 of our favorite deals from Amazon’s Pet Days sale
consumer_electronics.summary: A big part of my job here at The Verge is to find deals on tech that readers will like. But in my personal life, I’m not spending anywhere near as much money on tech as I am on food, litter, and other supplies for our two cats. Sad, but true. If your life is […]

consumer_electronics.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
consumer_electronics.summary: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

consumer_electronics.title: Bravo is creating unscripted microdramas for the Peacock app
consumer_electronics.summary: As microdrama apps like ReelShort and DramaBox quietly rake in billions, Peacock announced on Monday that it is launching two unscripted Bravo microdramas, which will stream in the Peacock app.

consumer_electronics.title: NYT’s Wordle to become a TV game show
consumer_electronics.summary: This will be the first time that The Times has collaborated with a TV broadcaster for an entertainment-based program, representing yet another pivot in the media company's attempt to build a sustainable digital subscription business as print revenue continues to decline.

consumer_electronics.summary: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

consumer_electronics.title: Venmo’s biggest makeover in years comes at a very interesting time
consumer_electronics.summary: The timing is notable. PayPal, which owns Venmo, is restructuring to spin Venmo off as a stand-alone business unit — a move widely seen as laying the groundwork for a potential sale. Stripe has reportedly expressed interest in buying PayPal outright.

channel_news.summary: Startup Made In is trying to carve out its defensive moat in the cookware space by focusing on what it calls "professional-grade cookware." This year, the company has entered one of the most formidable categories for cookware: cast iron. And it used the launch to add a new wholesale partner.

channel_news.title: Modern Retail+ Research: Marketers optimize GEO strategies amid the effects of zero-click search
channel_news.summary: A recent Modern Retail survey found that more than one-third of brand and agency pros (37%) said their companies have seen decreases in upper-funnel search traffic as a result of AI, and 21% said their companies have seen decreases in lower-funnel search traffic as a result of AI.

channel_news.title: How GameStop went from struggling retailer to eBay bidder
channel_news.summary: GameStop's ambitious bid to acquire eBay shows how, over the past six years, its CEO has repositioned the company from one struggling with lower physical video game software and hardware sales to one with far bigger ambitions, arguably beyond its scope.

channel_news.title: How Roller Rabbit became TikTok’s favorite sleepwear brand
channel_news.summary: Roller Rabbit became TikTok’s favorite pajama brand thanks to exclusive drops and collaborations with the likes of Target and Starbucks.

channel_news.title: The tradeoff economy: Why stressed shoppers are still spending
channel_news.summary: Katie Thomas from the Kearney Consumer Institute explains how shoppers' financial stress is showing up in their shopping habits.

channel_news.title: Havenly has been on an acquisition streak in the home space. Now, it’s launching a brand of its own
channel_news.summary: Havenly Brands is rolling out a new rug brand called Weft, the first internally incubated concept from the home-goods-focused house of brands.

channel_news.title: Marketplace Briefing: Temu wants to sell you a steak now
channel_news.summary: Temu, best known for cheap knick-knacks shipped directly from China, is now trying to convince shoppers to toss frozen ribeyes into the same cart as $3 phone cases and $5 dresses.

channel_news.title: Inside RadioShack’s audio-fueled B-to-B revival
channel_news.summary: Since mid-2024, RadioShack has been operating a wholesale consumer electronics business, where it sells portable speakers, batteries, radios and even turntables.

channel_news.title: Target’s new supply chain facility could be first of many
channel_news.summary: A new Target warehouse in Houston represents more than just a new building serving one region. It's potentially a new way in which the retailer's supply chain will operate throughout the U.S.

channel_news.title: Carter’s interim CEO: ‘We’re in line for our refund’
channel_news.summary: Richard Westenberger said that the company has filed for around $130 million in refunds from the government due to IEEPA-related tariff charges.

channel_news.title: Simon Property Group drops effort to evict Saks Global stores behind in rent
channel_news.summary: The parties have settled a dispute that threatened to derail progress in Saks’ effort to exit bankruptcy court next month.

channel_news.title: Bed Bath & Beyond Inc. subsidiary closing Tennessee office
channel_news.summary: The Brand House Collective, formerly known as Kirkland’s Home, will close its Brentwood office —  impacting 88 employees.

channel_news.summary: Peak isn’t about volume anymore, it’s about adaptability. Read the full perspective.
